title: Prathamesh Deshmukh | Physics PhD & Scientific Programmer
content: Prathamesh Keshao Deshmukh PhD Scholar | Condensed Matter Physics & Scientific Programmer I am a PhD researcher at the UGC-DAE Consortium for Scientific Research, Mumbai Centre, specializing in multiferroic composites. My work bridges experimental physics with computational science, focusing on magnetoelectric coupling and developing Python-based tools for lab automation to advance materials research. Academic Statistics 3 Publications 1 Citations 1 H-Index Social and Academic Profiles Google Scholar LinkedIn GitHub X (formerly Twitter) More social and academic links Research Profile Focusing on the intersection of magnetism and electricity in novel materials. Core Research: Multiferroic Composites My research is centered on fabricating and understanding materials where magnetic and electric properties are intrinsically linked. The goal is to contribute to next-generation sensors, memory devices, and energy-efficient electronics. Key Activities Novel material synthesis (e.g., BaFe10Sc2O19:KNbO3). Design of custom probes for cryogenic measurements. Structural analysis using Neutron & X-Ray Diffraction. Characterization of magnetic and electric properties. Explore my publications and detailed research work. Explore My Research Selected Publications Deshmukh, P., et al. (2024). "Exploration of near room temperature magnetoelectric coupling in BaFe10Sc2O19:KNbO3 composite." Journal of Physics and Chemistry of Solids, 196, 112309. View Article Mukherjee, S., et al. (2025). "Negative capacitance and magnetodielectric effect in Cu2O-CuO ceramics." Ceramics International, 51(20), 30716-30722. View Article Deshmukh, P., et al. (2025). "Exploring the role of Cu1+ in quasi-1D Cu1-xLixO (x = 0.025)." Journal of Alloys and Compounds, 1036, 181833. View Article View All Publications Academic & Research Journey A timeline of my educational and research milestones. May 2022 - Present PhD Scholar in Physics UGC-DAE Consortium for Scientific Research, Mumbai Centre 2019 - 2021 M.Sc in Physics Fergusson College, Savitribai Phule Pune University 2016 - 2019 B.Sc in Physics, Math, & Comp. App. Shri Shivaji Science College, SGB Amravati University Skills & Expertise Experimental Techniques Neutron Diffraction X-Ray Diffraction Dielectric Spectroscopy Magnetometry (VSM) UV-Vis Spectroscopy Sol-Gel Synthesis Materials Physics Lab Lab Facilities CSR User Portal Programming & Automation Developing custom Python scripts (PyVISA) to interface with and automate laboratory instruments for precise data acquisition. My flagship project, PICA, provides a full suite for this purpose. View All Projects PICA Project Analysis & Simulation Python (NumPy, SciPy) MATLAB COMSOL DFT Origin FullProf Suite Git Conferences & Workshops Attended: XIX School on Neutron as Probe of Condensed Matter, UGC-DAE CSR, Mumbai (Sep 2022) Poster Presentation: 66th DAE Solid State Physics Symposium, BIT Mesra, Ranchi (Dec 2022) Attended: 6th National Workshop on Materials Chemistry, Mumbai (Oct 2023) View All Presentations Get In Touch I'm open to discussing research, collaborations, or other opportunities. Feel free to reach out via email or connect with me on LinkedIn. For a quick query, you can also search the site. prathameshnium[at]duck[dot]com Mumbai, Maharashtra, India

title: Prathamesh Deshmukh | Computational Works
content: Computational Physics & Scientific Programming This portfolio showcases a collection of my projects focused on instrument control, data analysis, and simulation. Below, you will find highlights of my key projects, the technical tools I use, and links to my work. GitHub GitLab Google Scholar Email Technical Toolkit Core Programming Instrument Control Development & Docs Projects Flagship Project Lab Automation PICA: Python Instrument Control & Automation A comprehensive suite of Python scripts using PyVISA to control and automate instruments in our Materials Physics Lab for materials science research. Python PyVISA PyMeasure GUI Learn More View on GitHub Analysis & Simulation Toolkits Physics-Simulation-Toolkit A collection of scripts for simulating and visualizing phenomena in condensed matter physics, including the Ising model and dielectric models. Python Jupyter NumPy SciPy View on GitHub Solid-State-Physics-Calculators Scripts for analyzing experimental data, including calculations for activation energy (Arrhenius) and charge transport (Mott-VRH models). Python Pandas Matplotlib View on GitHub Python-for-OriginPro Scripts using the `originpro` library to automate plotting, data fitting, and report generation directly within OriginLab's software. Python originpro Pandas View on GitHub Code Collections & Archives Ketron An open-source knowledge base and archive of code snippets, experimental scripts, and small projects developed during my research. Python Jupyter LaTeX MATLAB View on GitHub Scientific-Python-Snippets A curated collection of small, reusable Python scripts for common data handling, file manipulation, and automation tasks. Python Tkinter Pandas View on GitHub Keywords & Expertise Scientific Computing Experimental Physics Data Analysis Python PyVISA Instrument Control Materials Science Multiferroics Condensed Matter Physics COMSOL Multiphysics Data Visualization Lab Automation NumPy Pandas SciPy

title: PICA: Python Instrument Control & Automation for Physics Research
content: PICA: Python-based Instrument Control and Automation A modular software suite for automating laboratory measurements in physics research. Project Overview PICA is a software suite designed to provide a robust framework for automating laboratory instruments in materials science and condensed matter physics research. The suite features a central GUI, the PICA Launcher, which serves as a dashboard for managing and executing a variety of characterization experiments. A key architectural feature is the use of isolated process execution for each measurement module via Python's `multiprocessing` library, ensuring high stability and preventing inter-script conflicts. Core Features Centralized Dashboard A comprehensive GUI for launching all modules. Isolated Processes Ensures stability and prevents resource conflicts. VISA Instrument Scanner Discover, identify, and troubleshoot connections. Modular Architecture Self-contained modules for each experiment. Embedded Docs In-app viewer for README and license files. System Console Log Real-time status updates and diagnostics. Available Measurement Modules Instrument Combination Measurement Type Description Keithley 6221 / 2182A I-V Characterization (AC Delta) High-precision I-V sweeps for low-resistance samples. Resistance vs. Temperature (R-T) Automated R-T data acquisition with active or passive temperature profiles. Keithley 2400 SourceMeter Four-Probe I-V Characterization Standard I-V sweeps for materials like semiconductors. Four-Probe R-T Characterization Temperature-dependent resistance measurements. Keithley 2400 / 2182A High-Precision I-V Enhanced voltage resolution using a nanovoltmeter. High-Precision R-T Temperature-dependent measurements with enhanced voltage precision. Keithley 6517B Electrometer High-Resistance I-V Characterization For insulating materials, dielectrics, and high-impedance devices. Resistivity vs. Temperature High-resistance measurements with active or passive temperature control. Pyroelectric Current vs. Temperature Quantifies pyroelectric current during a controlled temperature ramp. Keysight E4980A LCR Meter Capacitance-Voltage (C-V) Sweeps Automated C-V measurements for semiconductor and dielectric analysis. Lock-in Amplifier (Generic) AC Resistance Measurement For measuring AC transport properties and contact impedance. Lakeshore 340/350 Controller Temperature Control Utility A standalone module for defining and executing temperature profiles. Temperature Monitoring Utility A passive data logger for monitoring environmental temperature. Instrument Specifications Instrument System Parameter Range Best Resolution Keithley 6221/2182A Low Resistance Resistance ~10 nΩ to 200 MΩ Derived (from V/I) Current (6221) 100 fA to 105 mA 100 fA Voltage (2182A) 1 nV to 100 V 1 nV Keithley 2400 Mid-Range Resistance Resistance < 0.2 Ω to > 200 MΩ 100 µΩ Voltage (S/M) 5 µV / 1 µV to ±210 V 5 µV / 100 nV Current (S/M) 50 pA / 10 pA to ±1.05 A 50 pA / 10 pA Keithley 6517B High Resistance Resistance 1 Ω to > 10 PΩ Derived (from I) Current 10 aA to 21 mA 10 aA Voltage 1 µV to 210 V 1 µV Getting Started 1. Prerequisites Python 3.9 or newer. NI-VISA Driver for your operating system. 2. Installation Clone the repository: git clone https://github.com/prathameshnium/PICA-Python-Instrument-Control-and-Automation.git cd PICA-Python-Instrument-Control-and-Automation Install dependencies: pip install -r requirements.txt Launch the application: python PICA_Launcher.py Resources & Documentation A collection of official instrument manuals and a detailed technical guide on hardware setup and instrument configuration are available within the repository's `_assets/Manuals/` directory. Contributing Contributions are welcome! Please fork the repository, create a feature branch, and open a pull request. We recommend opening an issue first to discuss any major changes. Authors & Acknowledgments Lead Developer: Prathamesh Deshmukh Principal Investigator: Dr. Sudip Mukherjee UGC-DAE Consortium for Scientific Research, Mumbai Centre Funding: Financial support was provided under SERB-CRG project grant No. CRG/2022/005676 from the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF).
